triage.reasoning: Le contrat présente une vulnérabilité de réentrance dans la fonction withdraw, permettant à un attaquant d'extraire des fonds de manière répétée. Cette faille, déjà exploitée par le passé, représente un risque élevé de pertes financières significatives.

file_details.reasoning: Le contrat présente une vulnérabilité de réentrance dans la fonction withdraw, ce qui permet à un attaquant d'extraire des fonds de manière répétée avant que l'état du contrat ne soit mis à jour. Cette faille a déjà été exploitée dans le passé, ce qui augmente le risque de pertes financières significatives.

findings.description:
Reentrancy in EulerVault.withdraw(uint256) (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#22-33):
	External calls:
	- (ok,None) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}() (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#26)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- balances[msg.sender] -= amount (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#30)
	EulerVault.balances (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#11) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- EulerVault.balances (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#11)
	- EulerVault.deposit() (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#16-19)
	- EulerVault.withdraw(uint256) (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#22-33)

Reentrancy in EulerVault.withdraw(uint256) (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#22-33):
	External calls:
	- (ok,None) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}() (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#26)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- Withdrawal(msg.sender,amount) (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#32)

Low level call in EulerVault.withdraw(uint256) (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#22-33):
	- (ok,None) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}() (../contracts/contracts/Vulnerable/EulerVault.sol#26)

known_findings.description:
1. EulerVault has a vulnerability related to Reentrancy Attack.
2. There was a historical exploit on EulerVault in March 2022.
3. This vulnerability can lead to financial loss.
4. Reentrancy Attack exploits smart contracts.
5. EulerVault is used in Decentralized Finance (DeFi).

findings.title: Classic Reentrancy in withdraw() - External Call Before State Update
findings.reason: The withdraw() function executes msg.sender.call{value: amount}("") at line 26 before updating balances[msg.sender] -= amount at line 30, enabling an attacker to recursively call withdraw() and drain the contract.

findings.title: Missing Event Emission Before External Call
findings.reason: The Withdrawal event is emitted after the external call and state update, making it unreliable for off-chain monitoring during reentrancy attacks.
